title: Priority Slot Allocation for Arrival Flights
description: The system shall prioritize slot allocation to arrival flights over departure flights when allocating runway slots.
rationale: This requirement ensures that incoming aircraft can land safely and efficiently, reducing the risk of delays and conflicts with separation minima.
business_value: Improves operational efficiency by ensuring timely landing of arrival flights, enhancing safety for passengers and crew.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need arrival flights to be given priority over departure flights when allocating slots So that incoming aircraft can land safely and efficiently.

acceptance_criteria: All arrival flights are allocated slots before any departure flights are considered.
The arrival flight receives the slot when there is a conflict between an arrival and a departure flight requesting the same slot time.

If an arrival flight requests a slot that has already been allocated to a departure flight, the system reallocates the slot to the arrival flight and reassigns the departure flight to the next available slot.
preconditions: There is at least one runway slot available for allocation.
A set of both arrival flights and departure flights are competing for the same slot window.
postconditions: All arrival flights that requested slots have been allocated slots before any departure flights receive slots.
Any conflicts between arrival and departure flights have been resolved, with priority given to arrival flights.
functional_constraints: The system must ensure that safety constraints (e.g., separation minima) are not compromised during slot allocation.
All flights must have valid schedules, with no other overriding priorities affecting the allocation process.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should be able to handle requests for slot allocation efficiently without significant delays.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should maintain high uptime and reliability to ensure consistent performance under varying load conditions.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be designed to scale horizontally to accommodate an increasing number of flights and runway slots.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling mechanisms should be in place to manage unexpected situations gracefully, ensuring the user experience remains smooth.

edge_cases: What if there are no available runway slots for arrival flights?
How does the system handle multiple conflicting arrival and departure requests simultaneously?
What happens when a flight is canceled or rescheduled mid-allocation process?

interfaces.name: Runway Slot API
interfaces.description: The system will interact with this API to request and allocate runway slots.

interfaces.name: Flight Schedule Database
interfaces.description: This database will be used to retrieve current flight schedules and arrival/departure statuses.

interfaces.name: Safety Constraints Module
interfaces.description: The system will interface with this module to ensure all safety constraints are met during slot allocation.

test_cases.description: Verify that all arrival flights receive slots before any departure flights when there is an equal number of requests.
test_cases.expected_result: All arrival flights are allocated slots, and departure flights are put on a waiting list.

test_cases.description: Verify the system handles conflicts between an arrival and a departure flight requesting the same slot time.
test_cases.expected_result: The arrival flight receives the slot, and the departure flight is put on a waiting list.

test_cases.description: Verify that if an arrival flight requests a slot already allocated to a departure flight, the system reallocates the slot.
test_cases.expected_result: The arrival flight receives the previously occupied slot, and the departure flight is reassigned to the next available slot.
